system.name: Kinetic Gain Symptom Triage Assistant
system.description: Patient-facing chatbot that helps users decide whether their symptoms warrant a same-day visit, a virtual visit, or self-care. Informational only — does not make diagnoses, does not recommend specific medications, does not replace professional medical advice.

clinical_context.indication: Pre-encounter symptom triage for adult patients seeking guidance on whether and how to seek care. Indication scope: common acute symptoms (respiratory, GI, musculoskeletal, dermatologic). Not for emergencies — system immediately directs users to 911 or local emergency services when red-flag symptoms are detected.
clinical_context.intended_use: Patient-facing symptom triage and care-navigation assistant. Provides educational information and routes users to appropriate care channels. Does not provide diagnoses or treatment recommendations.

regulatory.notes: Vendor position: this system meets the 21st Century Cures Act §3060 CDS exclusion (does not analyze imaging / signals; provides recommendations the user can independently review; discloses underlying basis). FDA has issued enforcement-discretion guidance for general-wellness apps in this category. Vendor will reclassify if guidance changes.

validation_studies.title: Comparative concordance of Kinetic Gain Symptom Triage with board-certified triage nurses
validation_studies.primary_outcome: Agreement (kappa) between system recommendation and nurse triage recommendation across 4 acuity levels
validation_studies.results_summary: Weighted Cohen's kappa 0.74 (substantial agreement). System over-triaged (recommended higher acuity than nurse) 18% of the time; under-triaged 4%. Under-triage events were reviewed by clinical safety team; none involved missed life-threatening conditions.

training_data_sources: Annotated conversational transcripts (N=42k) from in-house triage-nurse consultations, 2022-2024, de-identified under Safe Harbor
Public symptom-checker datasets (Symptom Assessment Application Validation, SYMPTEMIST)

performance_metrics.measurement_population: US adult users self-referring to the triage chatbot, 2024-2025 (validation cohort)

escalation_protocols: Red-flag symptoms (chest pain, neuro deficit, severe bleeding, anaphylaxis, suicidal ideation) trigger an immediate full-screen takeover with 911 instructions; conversation pauses until user confirms.
Suicide-risk disclosures are escalated to a 988 warm-handoff workflow regardless of acuity level.
